summary: Tested PVP Auction Bidding Battle round transition fixes. Backend API tests (15/15 passed) verify all PVP endpoints including submit-round-result, tap-state, finish-round, and next-round. Frontend UI testing confirmed: 1) PVP Battle Arena loads correctly with all options, 2) Create Battle flow works with 5-photo selection UI, 3) Photo cards display correctly with values and stamina. Code review verified: round_winner_determined flag prevents duplicates, server sends round_selecting after 3s delay, handleRoundComplete correctly calculates scores based on isPlayer1.

minor.issue: 404 errors for some photo images (likely missing/deleted photos)

action_items: Real two-player WebSocket testing requires manual testing with two devices/browsers - cannot be automated in headless browser
Verify round transition works in real gameplay by having two users complete Round 1 and observe Round 2 transition
critical_code_review_comments: ✓ pvp_game_websocket.py line 89: round_winner_determined flag correctly initialized to False
✓ pvp_game_websocket.py line 952-956: submit_round_result checks and sets round_winner_determined to prevent duplicates
✓ pvp_game_websocket.py line 984-987: After 3s delay, increments round and calls _transition_to_selecting
✓ pvp_game_websocket.py line 676: round_selecting message broadcast with round, round_type, and scores
✓ PVPBattleArena.jsx line 355-371: round_selecting handler sets gamePhase to 'ready', resets states, increments round
✓ PVPBattleArena.jsx line 982-1091: handleRoundComplete correctly calculates newPlayer1Wins/newPlayer2Wins based on isPlayer1 and winner
✓ game_routes.py line 2063-2145: API fallback /pvp/submit-round-result works when WebSocket unavailable

success_rate.frontend: 100% (UI loads correctly, Create Battle flow works)

context_for_next_testing_agent: PVP round transition logic has been verified at code level. The key fixes are: 1) round_winner_determined flag prevents duplicate submissions, 2) Server sends round_selecting after 3s delay to transition to next round, 3) handleRoundComplete correctly calculates scores based on isPlayer1. Real two-player testing with WebSocket requires manual testing with two browsers/devices.

code_verification.round_transition_flow: handleRoundComplete → WS round_result → server submit_round_result → 3s delay → round_selecting → frontend ready phase
code_verification.duplicate_prevention: round_winner_determined flag checked before processing, set to true during processing, reset to false for next round
code_verification.score_calculation: currentUserWon = winner === 'player'; if currentUserWon && isPlayer1 → player1Wins++; if currentUserWon && !isPlayer1 → player2Wins++; etc.

note: The fixes appear correctly implemented. The main limitation is that real two-player WebSocket testing cannot be performed in automated headless browser environment. Manual testing with two devices/browsers is recommended to verify the round transition works in actual gameplay.
